justification: Both terminology mismatches identified (session/token and suspended/locked). terminology_alignment section explicitly maps both pairs. Brief uses consistent terminology with clear notes on origin (Bird=domain, MJ=technical). Both ACs present with exact error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED preserved. Architecture details included: Redis TTL 1800, last_accessed_at field, token issuance check for locked accounts. The suspended/locked mismatch is escalated to Bird with options rather than silently resolved, which exceeds the rubric requirement.

observations.text: terminology_alignment section explicitly maps both pairs: session→token and suspended→locked
Escalation correctly flags that suspended/locked may be distinct states rather than assuming synonymy
Both error codes SESSION_EXPIRED and ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED preserved exactly
Redis TTL 1800, last_accessed_at, and token issuance check all present in architecture guidance
Clear instruction to Shaq to NOT implement account status field name until Bird confirms — prevents downstream confusion
Raises valuable open question about whether account status should be rechecked on every request vs only at login
